reasoning: A major military crisis between India and Pakistan occurred in April–May 2025 following the April 22 Pahalgam attack. This included cross-border airstrikes and missile/drone exchanges. However, a US-brokered ceasefire was agreed upon on May 10, 2025. Crucially, the resolution criteria require a *formal declaration of war*. Even during the peak of this unprecedented recent escalation, neither side formally declared war, instead seeking diplomatic offramps and explicitly avoiding a formal declaration (which is standard practice in post-WWII conflicts under international law; the last formal declaration between the two was in 1971). While border skirmishes may persist, a formal declaration of war before the end of 2025 remains highly improbable. The 18% market price on May 15 was likely driven by peak-crisis panic and bettors neglecting the strict 'formal declaration' requirement (which the market creator explicitly clarified on May 8).
